name: Stored Username Attack
description: Registration is safely parameterized, but a password reset feature unsafely uses the stored username in a query. Register a malicious username, then trigger the second-order injection via password reset.
briefing: CloudVault, a file hosting service, has a registration and password reset flow. The registration endpoint uses parameterized queries — direct injection won't work. However, the password reset feature retrieves the stored username from the database and concatenates it into an UPDATE query. Register an account with a carefully crafted username, then trigger the password reset to execute the second-order injection. The 'vault_secrets' table holds the flag.
objective: Register a user with a malicious username, then exploit the password reset to perform second-order SQL injection and extract the flag.
author: h4ck0rman
